findings.evidence: The new definition is exactly `fixingSubgroup ... (I.boundary M)` at lines 48-49, while Mathlib's source has `def fixingSubgroup (s : Set α)` and `theorem mem_fixingSubgroup_iff {s : Set α}` in `Mathlib/GroupTheory/GroupAction/FixingSubgroup.lean`; the PR description also names the set-parametric `Diffeomorph.fixedOnSubgroup I n s`.
findings.fix: Add `fixedOnSubgroup (s : Set M) : Subgroup (M ≃ₘ^n⟮I, I⟯ M) := fixingSubgroup (M ≃ₘ^n⟮I, I⟯ M) s`, prove `mem_fixedOnSubgroup_iff` and the forgetful compatibility for arbitrary `s`, then define `boundaryFixingSubgroup` and its lemmas as special cases.
findings.issue: `boundaryFixingSubgroup` is the only named subgroup, but the natural API is the relative subgroup for any `s : Set M`; the boundary case is just one specialization.

summary: The new subgroup API is under-generalized: it only exposes the boundary case even though the construction and proofs are naturally for an arbitrary fixed subset.
